profile.name: Chengzhi Mao
profile.meta_description: Chengzhi Mao — Assistant Professor, Rutgers CS. Research in LLMs, Computer Vision, and Controllable Perception.
profile.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Computer Science, Rutgers University

bio.heading: Brief Bio
paragraphs: I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science at Rutgers University. My research spans large (vision) language models, computer vision, and AI trustworthiness.

Previously, I have been a Research Scientist at Google, working on Gemini and GenAI related research. I have been a Core Faculty Member at MILA, and an Assistant Professor at McGill. I completed my Ph.D. at Columbia University in 2023, advised by Carl Vondrick and Junfeng Yang. I got my BS at Tsinghua University in 2018, advised by Yuan Shen. I was also a visiting student at MIT, advised by Dina Katabi.

I am actively recruiting highly self-motivated Ph.D. students, research interns, and postdocs. If you’re interested, please email me with your CV. Interest and experience in LLM thinking, 3D, robotics, and diffusion models will be a plus.

items.html: Congratulations to Qihao and collaborators! Our paper on LLM-based model auditing has been selected as a Highlight.
Congratulations to Zirui Zhang and Yang Li on their summer research internships at Google Research and Google DeepMind, respectively.
Organizing the CVPR 2026 Workshop ActiVis: Bridging Vision, Language, and Action for Robotics. See call for papers for submission.
Congratulations to Zirui and Haoyu's CVPR 2026 paper on multimodal reinforcement learning.
Congratulations to Arijit's CVPR 2026 Findings paper on multimodal latent reasoning.
One paper on controllable perception accepted to ICLR 2026.
Serving as Area Chair for ECCV 2026.
Serving as Area Chair for ICML 2026.
Giving an invited talk at ICCV 2025. The title is "Seeing Through Words: Understanding and Controlling Vision Foundation Models via Language."
Serving as Area Chair for ICLR 2026.
Serving as Area Chair for NeurIPS 2025.
Serving as Area Chair for ICLR 2025.
Received the Discovery Grant for NSERC.
